items.text:
SCHOOL LIBRARY
All books must be returned by Friday.
Students who keep books longer will pay a small fine.
items.prompt: What must students do by Friday?
items.rationale: The notice says books must be returned by Friday.

items.text: Hey! Football practice is cancelled today because the pitch is wet. We'll play on Thursday instead. Don't forget your kit!
items.prompt: Why is football practice cancelled?
items.rationale: The message says practice is cancelled because the pitch is wet.

items.text:
CANTEEN HOURS
Breakfast: 7:30–9:00
Lunch: 12:00–14:00
No food served outside these times.
items.prompt: When can students get food at school?
items.rationale: The sign gives fixed hours — students cannot get food outside those times.

items.text:
Hi everyone,
The science fair will be on 20 March. Please bring your projects by 8:30 am. Projects must fit on a normal desk.
items.prompt: What does the email say about projects?
items.rationale: The email says projects must be brought by 8:30 am.

items.text:
City Youth Sports Club
New members welcome!
First month FREE
Bring a friend and both get 10% off
items.prompt: What offer does the club give new members?
items.rationale: The website says the first month is free for new members.

items.text:
LOST: One blue school bag
Found near the gym on Monday morning
Please collect from school office.
items.prompt: Where can the owner pick up the bag?
items.rationale: The notice says the bag can be collected from the school office.

items.text: Mum, I forgot my PE kit. Can you bring it to school before noon? My lesson is at 2 pm. Thanks!
items.prompt: Why does the student contact their mum?
opts: To ask her to watch the PE lesson
To tell her the lesson time changed
To ask her to bring forgotten kit
items.rationale: The student forgot their PE kit and needs mum to bring it.

items.text:
SUMMER CODING CAMP
Learn to make apps and games
Ages 12–16
Monday–Friday, 9 am–3 pm
Limited places — book now!
items.prompt: Who is the coding camp for?
items.rationale: The advertisement says the camp is for ages 12–16.

items.text:
DRAMA CLUB
Join us every Wednesday after school
No experience needed
First session is free
Room 14
items.prompt: What does the notice say about experience?
items.rationale: The notice says no experience is needed.

items.text: The homework for tomorrow has changed. Mr Torres says we only need to do exercises 3 and 4 now, not the whole page.
items.prompt: What has changed about the homework?
opts: Students must do less than before
The homework is due next week
items.rationale: Students now only do exercises 3 and 4 instead of the whole page.

items.text:
COMPUTER ROOM
Please log out before you leave.
Food and drinks are not allowed inside.
items.prompt: What is NOT allowed in the computer room?
items.rationale: The sign says food and drinks are not allowed inside the computer room.

items.text:
Hi Lena,
I can't meet you at the bus stop this morning. I have a dentist appointment. I'll see you in class at 9.
items.prompt: Where will the writer see Lena?
items.rationale: The note says the writer will see Lena in class at 9.

items.text:
SCHOOL PHOTO DAY — Thursday 10 October
Wear your school uniform.
Photos taken in the main hall between 9 am and 12 pm.
items.prompt: Where will the photos be taken?
items.rationale: The advertisement says photos will be taken in the main hall.

items.text:
Dear students,
The school trip to the museum has been moved to Friday 15 November. Please give your permission slips to your teacher by Wednesday.
items.prompt: What has changed about the school trip?
items.rationale: The email says the trip has been moved to Friday 15 November.

items.text: Hi Tom! I'm on holiday in Spain. The weather is hot and sunny every day. I tried paella for the first time — it was delicious! Wish you were here. — Anya
items.prompt: How does Anya describe the weather in Spain?
items.rationale: Anya says the weather is hot and sunny every day.

items.text:
SCHOOL DANCE — Friday evening
Tickets: £3 each
Buy your ticket from the school office before Thursday 5 pm.
No tickets sold at the door.
items.prompt: What is the price of a ticket for the dance?
items.rationale: The notice says tickets cost £3 each.

items.text: Dad, can you pick me up at 5:30 instead of 5? Rehearsal is running late. I'll wait outside the main entrance.
items.prompt: Where will the student wait for their dad?
items.rationale: The student says they will wait outside the main entrance.

items.text:
Art materials
Handle with care — glass bottles inside.
Store in a cool, dry place.
Keep away from young children.
items.prompt: Why should people handle this box carefully?
items.rationale: The label says to handle with care because there are glass bottles inside.

items.text:
Oak Hill Swimming Pool
Open to schools Tuesday and Thursday mornings only.
All swimmers must bring their own towel.
Showers available.
items.prompt: What must all swimmers bring to the pool?
items.rationale: The website says all swimmers must bring their own towel.

items.text:
STAFF CAR PARK
Students must not enter this area.
Bicycles must be left at the front entrance.
items.prompt: Who is not allowed in the staff car park?
items.rationale: The sign says students must not enter the staff car park.

items.text: Jake — I borrowed your maths book to copy the notes I missed. I'll return it tomorrow morning before class. Sorry for not asking first! — Dan
items.prompt: When will Dan return the book?
items.rationale: Dan says he will return the book tomorrow morning before class.

items.text:
SECOND-HAND BOOK SALE
This Saturday in the school gym
10 am – 1 pm
All books 50p or less — great for holiday reading!
items.prompt: Where is the book sale taking place?
items.rationale: The advertisement says the book sale is in the school gym.

items.text:
Hi Sam,
My train gets in at 7:15 pm on Saturday. Could you meet me at the station? I have two heavy bags. Thanks! — Rosa
items.prompt: What time does Rosa's train arrive?
items.rationale: Rosa says her train gets in at 7:15 pm.

items.text: Hello from Paris! We visited the Eiffel Tower yesterday — it was amazing. Today we're going to a French cookery class. See you next week! — Mia
items.prompt: What did Mia visit yesterday?
items.rationale: Mia says they visited the Eiffel Tower yesterday.

items.text:
SCHOOL GARDEN PROJECT
We need volunteers to help plant vegetables every Saturday morning.
No gardening experience necessary.
Meet at the back gate at 9 am.
items.prompt: Where should volunteers meet on Saturday?
items.rationale: The notice says volunteers should meet at the back gate at 9 am.

items.text: Hi! Are you coming to Jess's birthday party on Saturday? It starts at 3 pm. Her mum says we can stay until 8. Bring a small gift if you can!
items.prompt: Until what time can guests stay at the party?
items.rationale: The message says guests can stay until 8.

items.text:
SPORTS HALL
Trainers must be worn at all times.
No outdoor shoes.
Visitors must sign in at reception.
items.prompt: What must visitors do before using the sports hall?
items.rationale: The sign says visitors must sign in at reception.

items.text:
Chocolate chip cookies
Contains: wheat, milk, eggs, nuts.
Best before: 12 August.
Store in a cool dry place.
items.prompt: Until when can the cookies be eaten?
items.rationale: The label says the best before date is 12 August.

items.text:
Greenfield Youth Theatre
Auditions for our summer show are open to ages 11–18.
No previous acting experience needed.
Register online by 30 June.
items.prompt: What is the last date to register for auditions?
items.rationale: The website says applicants must register online by 30 June.

items.text:
AFTER-SCHOOL ART CLASS
Every Tuesday 3:30–5:00 pm
For students aged 13 and above
Materials provided — just bring yourself!
Sign up in the art room.
items.prompt: What do students need to bring to the art class?
items.rationale: The advertisement says materials are provided, so students just need to bring themselves.

items.text:
SCHOOL ART EXHIBITION
Year 7 and Year 8 students can show their artwork in the main hall.
Bring your paintings or drawings to Room 12 before Thursday.
items.prompt: What should students bring to Room 12?
items.rationale: The notice says students should bring their paintings or drawings to Room 12.

items.text:
SPRING CONCERT — FRIDAY 7PM
Tickets: £3 for students, £5 for adults.
Buy your tickets at the school office before Wednesday.
items.prompt: Where can students buy concert tickets?
items.rationale: The advertisement says to buy tickets at the school office.

items.text:
SCHOOL DANCE — SATURDAY 7PM
Dress code: smart casual. No trainers allowed.
Tickets cost £4 and must be bought in advance.
items.prompt: What are students NOT allowed to wear to the dance?
items.rationale: The poster says no trainers are allowed at the dance.

items.text:
CHARITY BAKE SALE
All cakes and biscuits made by students.
All money goes to the local children's hospital.
Sale starts at lunchtime in the main hall.
items.prompt: Why is the bake sale being held?
opts: To raise money for a hospital
To celebrate the end of term
items.rationale: The sign says all money goes to the local children's hospital.

items.text:
TALENT SHOW SIGN-UPS OPEN!
Do you sing, dance, or play an instrument? Sign up at the main office by next Monday.
The show will take place on the last day of term.
items.prompt: When is the deadline to sign up for the talent show?
items.rationale: The announcement says sign up by next Monday.

items.text:
SPORTS EQUIPMENT RENTAL
Students can borrow bats, balls, and skipping ropes at lunchtime.
Leave your student card at the office to borrow equipment.
All items must be returned before 1:30pm.
items.prompt: What must students leave at the office to borrow equipment?
items.rationale: The notice says students must leave their student card at the office.

items.text:
BICYCLE PARKING
Please lock your bicycle in the area behind the sports hall.
Do not leave bikes near the main entrance.
The school is not responsible for lost or stolen bicycles.
items.prompt: Where should students park their bicycles?
items.rationale: The sign says to lock bicycles in the area behind the sports hall.

items.text:
Hi Lily,
We need three more volunteers for the school garden this Saturday morning.
We will plant new flowers and water the vegetable beds. Can you come?
Mrs Hill
items.prompt: What will volunteers do in the garden on Saturday?
items.rationale: The email says they will plant new flowers and water the vegetable beds.

items.text:
COMPUTER LAB BOOKING
Students must book the computer lab at least one day in advance.
Groups of more than four need a teacher to be present.
Booking forms are available from the library.
items.prompt: How far in advance must students book the computer lab?
items.rationale: The notice says students must book at least one day in advance.

items.text:
LIBRARY NEW ARRIVALS — JUNE
We have 20 new books this month, including adventure stories and science books.
All new books are on the display shelf near the entrance.
You can borrow up to two new arrivals at a time.
items.prompt: Where are the new library books kept?
opts: On the display shelf near the entrance
items.rationale: The website says all new books are on the display shelf near the entrance.

items.text:
CAFETERIA ALLERGY WARNING
This dish contains NUTS.
Please speak to a member of staff if you have a food allergy before eating.
items.prompt: What should students with food allergies do?
opts: Speak to a member of staff
items.rationale: The label says students with allergies should speak to a member of staff.

items.text:
SCHOOL NEWSPAPER — CALL FOR WRITERS!
Are you interested in writing for our school newspaper?
Send your first article to Mr. Davies in Room 5 by the end of this week.
items.prompt: Where should students send their articles?
items.rationale: The announcement says to send articles to Mr. Davies in Room 5.

items.text:
JOIN THE DRAMA CLUB!
Meetings every Tuesday at 4pm in the school theatre.
No experience needed — everyone is welcome.
Speak to Miss Carter to join.

items.rationale: The poster says students should speak to Miss Carter to join.

items.text:
DEBATE TEAM TRYOUTS
Do you enjoy talking about important topics?
Tryouts for the school debate team will be held on Wednesday at 3:30pm in Room 8.
All year groups are welcome to attend.
items.prompt: When are the debate team tryouts?
items.rationale: The notice says tryouts will be held on Wednesday at 3:30pm.

items.text:
CHESS CLUB
Every Thursday lunchtime in Room 3.
Beginners and experienced players welcome.
Bring your own set if you have one.
items.prompt: Where does chess club take place?
items.rationale: The sign says chess club is in Room 3.

items.text:
Hi Sam,
Film club is showing a new French film next Friday. It has English subtitles.
Can you bring some snacks for everyone? The room fits about 20 people.
Jake
items.prompt: What does Jake ask Sam to bring?
items.rationale: Jake asks Sam to bring some snacks for everyone.

items.text:
LANGUAGE EXCHANGE PROGRAMME
Do you want to practise your Spanish or French?
Meet with a partner every Wednesday at 4pm in the library.
Contact Miss Vega to register your interest.
items.prompt: What is the purpose of the language exchange programme?
opts: To learn new languages in class
To practise Spanish or French with a partner
items.rationale: The poster says the programme is to practise Spanish or French.

items.text:
SCHOOL TRIP ABROAD — IMPORTANT REMINDER
All students going to Paris must bring their passport to school by this Friday.
Students without a passport cannot travel.
See Mrs. Brown if you have any questions.
items.prompt: What must students bring to school by Friday?
items.rationale: The notice says all students going to Paris must bring their passport by Friday.

items.text: Hi! Don't forget — end-of-year party is on Thursday at 6pm in the sports hall. Wear something colourful! Let me know if you need a lift. — Priya
items.prompt: What does Priya tell the reader to wear to the party?
items.rationale: Priya says to wear something colourful to the party.

items.text:
SPORTS TROPHY CEREMONY
The end-of-year sports awards will be held on Friday at 3pm in the main hall.
Parents and family members are welcome to attend.
Refreshments will be served after the ceremony.
items.prompt: When will the sports trophy ceremony take place?
items.rationale: The announcement says the awards will be held on Friday at 3pm.

items.text:
QUIZ NIGHT — THIS FRIDAY!
Teams of four students. Questions on sport, science, music, and films.
Entry is free. Winners get a £20 book voucher.
Doors open at 6:30pm in the library.
items.prompt: How much does it cost to enter the quiz night?
items.rationale: The poster says entry is free.

items.text:
HOMEWORK CLUB
Get help with your homework every Monday and Wednesday from 3:30–4:30pm.
A teacher will be available to answer questions.
Room 14. No need to book — just come along.
items.prompt: How do students join homework club?
opts: They just come along — no booking needed
They must book a place in advance
They need a teacher to sign them in
items.rationale: The notice says no need to book — students just come along.

items.text:
SUMMER READING CHALLENGE
Read 6 books this summer and get a special certificate!
Visit the local library to register before the end of July.
All children aged 6–12 can join.
items.prompt: Where must children go to register for the reading challenge?
items.rationale: The advertisement says to visit the local library to register.

items.text:
SPELLING COMPETITION — SIGN UP NOW!
The school spelling competition will take place on 14th March.
All students from Years 5–8 can enter.
Sign up with your form teacher by next Friday.
items.prompt: What must students do to enter the spelling competition?
opts: Sign up with their form teacher
items.rationale: The announcement says students must sign up with their form teacher.

items.text:
SCIENCE EXPERIMENT SAFETY
All students must wear safety glasses during experiments.
Do not touch chemicals without gloves.
Report any accidents immediately to your teacher.
items.prompt: What must students wear during science experiments?
items.rationale: The notice says all students must wear safety glasses during experiments.

items.text: Hey, don't forget your PE kit tomorrow! We have football in the morning and swimming after lunch. See you then! — Tom
items.prompt: Why does Tom send this message?
opts: To remind the reader to bring their PE kit
items.rationale: Tom sends the message to remind the reader to bring their PE kit.

items.text:
SCHOOL COUNCIL ELECTIONS
Vote for your class representative next Tuesday.
Any student in Years 7–9 can stand as a candidate.
Nomination forms are in the main office.
items.prompt: Where can students get nomination forms?
items.rationale: The poster says nomination forms are available in the main office.

items.text:
WATER BOTTLES
Students may bring water bottles to class.
Only water is allowed — no juice or fizzy drinks.
All bottles must have a lid to prevent spills.
items.prompt: What must all water bottles have?
items.rationale: The sign says all bottles must have a lid to prevent spills.

items.text:
MOBILE PHONE POLICY
Phones must be switched off and kept in your bag during lessons.
You may use your phone at break and lunchtime only.
Phones found in use during lessons will be taken by teachers.
items.prompt: What happens to phones used during lessons?
items.rationale: The notice says phones found in use during lessons will be taken by teachers.

items.text:
SCHOOL SHOP — OPEN MONDAYS AND FRIDAYS
Buy pens, pencils, rulers, and notebooks at low prices.
We also sell school badges and ties.
Open at lunchtime only.
items.prompt: When is the school shop open?
opts: At lunchtime on Mondays and Fridays
items.rationale: The advertisement says the shop is open at lunchtime on Mondays and Fridays.

items.text:
LOST PROPERTY
Have you lost something at school?
All lost items are kept in the box outside the main office.
Items not collected by Friday will be given to charity.
items.prompt: Where are lost items kept?
opts: In a box outside the main office
items.rationale: The notice says lost items are kept in the box outside the main office.

items.text: Hi! My birthday party is on Saturday at 3pm. It's at my house — 14 Oak Street. Please let me know if you can come by Thursday! — Mia
items.prompt: Where is Mia's birthday party?
items.rationale: Mia says the party is at her house at 14 Oak Street.

items.text: Hi Amir — if you stay for the sleepover, bring your sleeping bag, toothbrush, and something to read. Lights out at 10pm. No screens after 9:30pm. See you Saturday! — Dan
items.prompt: What time do the lights go out at the sleepover?
items.rationale: The note says lights out at 10pm.

items.text:
PET SHOW — COME AND JOIN IN!
Bring your pet to school on 20th June.
All animals welcome — dogs, cats, rabbits, and more!
Prizes for the friendliest and most unusual pet.
items.prompt: What kinds of animals can students bring to the pet show?
items.rationale: The poster says all animals are welcome — dogs, cats, rabbits, and more.

items.text:
Dear parents and students,
Our school is organising a community clean-up day on Saturday 8th July from 9am–12pm.
Students who join will receive a certificate. Please bring gloves and wear old clothes.
Mr. Patel
items.prompt: What should students wear to the community clean-up day?
items.rationale: The email says students should wear old clothes to the clean-up day.

items.text: Cinema — Last entry for evening shows is 15 minutes before the film starts.
items.prompt: What does the sign tell you?
opts: You must arrive at least 15 minutes before the film
You cannot buy tickets after the film begins
Evening shows start 15 minutes later than usual
items.rationale: The sign says the last entry is 15 minutes before the film starts, so you must arrive at least that early.

items.text: GRAND CAFÉ — Today's special: pasta with tomato sauce and salad. €8.50 including a drink.
items.prompt: What does the notice tell customers?
opts: Pasta is always on the menu at this price
The café is offering a meal deal today
items.rationale: The notice announces a special meal deal available today that includes food and a drink.

items.text: SUPERMARKET — We are open Monday–Saturday 08:00–21:00 and Sunday 10:00–17:00.
items.prompt: What does the sign tell shoppers?
opts: The supermarket is open every day of the week
The supermarket is closed on Sundays
The supermarket closes at the same time every day
items.rationale: The sign shows opening hours for all seven days, so the supermarket is open every day.

items.text: City Gym — Join now and pay nothing for the first month! Full use of all equipment and classes included.
items.prompt: What is the gym offering new members?
opts: Free equipment to keep when you join
A free month with access to everything
items.rationale: The advertisement says new members pay nothing for the first month and get full access.

items.text: Bus Route 14 — Due to road works, this bus will not stop at Market Street from Monday to Friday this week.
items.prompt: What does the notice tell passengers?
opts: One stop is being skipped on weekdays this week
The bus timetable is changing permanently
items.rationale: The notice explains that Market Street will not be a stop from Monday to Friday this week only.

items.text: PLATFORM 3 — This platform is for trains to the city centre only. For all other destinations, please use platforms 1 or 2.
items.prompt: What does the sign tell travellers going to other destinations?
opts: They need to go to platform 1 or 2
They must check the information board first
items.rationale: The sign tells travellers going anywhere other than the city centre to use platforms 1 or 2.

items.text: Attention passengers on flight BT204 to Madrid. Your departure gate has changed from Gate 12 to Gate 19. Please make your way there now.
items.prompt: What must passengers on flight BT204 do now?
opts: Wait at Gate 12 for more information
Check in again at the departure desk
items.rationale: The announcement tells passengers the gate has changed and they must go to Gate 19 now.

items.text: Hotel Sunrise — Check-in is from 15:00. If you arrive before this time, we are happy to store your luggage.
items.prompt: What can guests do if they arrive at the hotel before 15:00?
opts: Leave their bags with the hotel
items.rationale: The notice says the hotel will store luggage for guests who arrive before check-in time.

items.text: CITY PARK — Gates open daily at 07:00 and close at dusk. Dogs must be kept on leads at all times.
items.prompt: What does the sign say about dogs in the park?
opts: Dogs are not allowed in the park
Dogs may run free in certain areas
Dogs must always be on a lead
items.rationale: The sign states that dogs must be kept on leads at all times in the park.

items.text: CITY MUSEUM — Free entry every first Sunday of the month for all visitors. Normal ticket prices apply on other days.
items.prompt: When can visitors enter the museum for free?
opts: On the first Sunday of each month
items.rationale: The poster says free entry is available on the first Sunday of each month.

items.text: Sports Centre Pool — All lane bookings must be made online at least 24 hours in advance. No walk-in bookings accepted.
items.prompt: How must swimmers book a lane at the sports centre?
opts: Online, the day before or earlier
In person at the reception desk
items.rationale: The notice says bookings must be made online at least 24 hours in advance.

items.text: ICE RINK — Skaters must not hold hands across lanes. Always skate in the same direction as other skaters.
items.prompt: What does the sign ask skaters to do?
opts: Skate in the opposite direction to warm up
Stay in the outer lane at all times
Move in the same direction as other skaters
items.rationale: The sign says skaters must always move in the same direction as others.

items.text: SUPER BOWL — Children under 8 must be supervised by an adult at all times. Please return hired shoes before you leave.
items.prompt: What must visitors do when they finish bowling?
opts: Pay for the shoes at the exit
items.rationale: The notice asks visitors to return hired shoes before they leave.

items.text: GO-KART TRACK — Drivers must be at least 12 years old. All drivers must wear a helmet. No overtaking in the pit lane.
items.prompt: What is not allowed on the go-kart track?
items.rationale: The sign says no overtaking is allowed in the pit lane.

items.text: ADVENTURE WORLD — Ride: The Dragon. Minimum height 1.20 m. Children below this height will not be able to ride.
items.prompt: Who cannot go on The Dragon ride?
items.rationale: The notice says children below 1.20 m in height cannot go on the ride.

items.text: BEACH — Red flag flying means swimming is dangerous. Do not enter the water when the red flag is up.
items.prompt: What does a red flag at the beach mean?
opts: It is too dangerous to swim
Swimming is allowed in marked areas
items.rationale: The sign says the red flag means swimming is dangerous and people should not enter the water.

items.text: LAKESIDE CAMPSITE — Campfires are not permitted on the site. Campers may use the BBQ area near the main building.
items.prompt: What does the notice tell campers about fires?
opts: Campfires are banned but a BBQ area is available
Campfires are only allowed at weekends
No cooking of any kind is permitted on site
items.rationale: The notice says campfires are not permitted but there is a BBQ area available near the main building.

items.text: HILLTOP YOUTH HOSTEL — Quiet hours are between 22:00 and 07:00. Please do not disturb other guests during these times.
items.prompt: What are guests asked to do between 22:00 and 07:00?
opts: Be quiet so as not to disturb others
items.rationale: The sign asks guests not to disturb others during the quiet hours of 22:00 to 07:00.

items.text: SEAVIEW APARTMENT — No parties or loud music after 22:00. Rubbish must be placed in the bins provided outside.
items.prompt: What does the notice say about rubbish?
opts: It must be left in the kitchen
It must go in the outside bins
items.rationale: The notice says rubbish must be placed in the bins provided outside.

items.text: Tourist Information Centre — Free maps, local guides, and event listings available inside. Ask a member of staff for help.
items.prompt: What can visitors pick up free at the tourist information centre?
items.rationale: The label says free maps, local guides, and event listings are available inside the centre.

items.text: Hi Sam, just a reminder — your travel insurance runs out on 30 June. You need to renew it before your holiday on 5 July. — Mum
items.prompt: Why is Mum writing to Sam?
opts: To remind Sam to renew the travel insurance
To say the holiday has been cancelled
items.rationale: Mum is reminding Sam that the travel insurance expires before the holiday and needs to be renewed.

items.text: STYLE ZONE — This weekend only: 20% off everything in store for customers who show their birthday card. Happy shopping!
items.prompt: Who gets a discount at Style Zone this weekend?
opts: Customers who show a birthday card
items.rationale: The advertisement offers 20% off to customers who show a birthday card, this weekend only.

items.text: MEGA SPORTS — SUMMER SALE — Up to 50% off selected items. Sale ends Sunday. While stocks last.
items.prompt: What does the poster tell shoppers about the sale?
opts: All items are half price until Sunday
Chosen items are discounted and the sale finishes Sunday
The store will close after Sunday
items.rationale: The poster says selected items are up to 50% off and the sale ends on Sunday.

items.text: Your order #7823 has been confirmed. Estimated delivery: Thursday 10–12am. You will receive a message when it is on its way. — ShopFast
items.prompt: What does the message tell the customer?
opts: The order has not gone through
Another message will be sent when the parcel is dispatched
The customer must confirm the delivery time
items.rationale: The message says the customer will receive another message when the parcel is on its way.

items.text: Delivery note: We tried to deliver your parcel today but you were out. Please collect it from the post office on Green Street. It will be kept for 14 days.
items.prompt: What should the person do to get their parcel?
opts: Wait at home for another delivery attempt tomorrow
Phone the delivery company to rearrange delivery
Go to the post office on Green Street to collect it
items.rationale: The note says the parcel must be collected from the post office on Green Street.

items.text: WARNING: Keep away from children under 3 years. Contains small parts which may cause choking.
items.prompt: Why does the label warn about small children?
opts: The product is too noisy for babies
The small parts could be a choking danger
Children under 3 can break the product easily
items.rationale: The label warns that the product contains small parts that could cause choking in children under 3.

items.text: EASY COOK PASTA — Boil water, add pasta, and cook for 10 minutes. Drain and serve. For best results, do not overcook.
items.prompt: What is the first step when cooking this pasta?
opts: Add the pasta to cold water
items.rationale: The instructions say to boil the water first, then add the pasta.

items.text: PAINKILLER TABLETS — Adults: take 1 or 2 tablets every 4–6 hours. Do not take more than 8 tablets in 24 hours. Not suitable for children under 12.
items.prompt: Who should not take these tablets according to the label?
items.rationale: The label says the tablets are not suitable for children under 12.

items.text: FRESHBAKE BREAD — Contains wheat, nuts, and sesame seeds. May also contain traces of milk. Suitable for vegetarians.
items.prompt: What does the notice warn people about?
opts: The bread may not be fresh
The bread is not suitable for vegetarians
The bread contains nuts and may have traces of milk
items.rationale: The notice lists nuts as an ingredient and says the bread may also contain traces of milk.

items.text: COOK IT! — Beginners' cooking classes every Saturday at 10:00. Limited places — book online or call 555-0192.
items.prompt: How can people reserve a place on the cooking class?
opts: By arriving early on Saturday morning
By booking online or by phone
By visiting the studio in person
items.rationale: The poster says people can book online or by calling 555-0192.

items.text: MAKE & CREATE STUDIO — Saturday craft workshop for ages 8–14. Make your own clay pot! Tickets £12. Bring an old T-shirt.
items.prompt: What should participants bring to the workshop?
items.rationale: The advertisement tells participants to bring an old T-shirt to the workshop.

items.text: YOUNG PHOTOGRAPHERS — Annual competition now open! Send up to 3 photos by 31 August. Theme: Nature. Open to under-18s only.
items.prompt: What is the closing date for the photography competition?
items.rationale: The poster says photos must be sent by 31 August.

items.text: BOOKWORMS CLUB — Monthly meeting this Thursday at 18:30 in the library reading room. This month's book: 'The Blue River'. All welcome.
items.prompt: Where will the book club meeting take place?
items.rationale: The notice says the meeting will be held in the library reading room.

items.text: You are invited to a special screening of 'Journey to the Stars' on Friday 28 June at 19:00, Room B, Community Centre. Free entry. Light refreshments served.
items.prompt: How much does it cost to attend the film screening?
items.rationale: The invitation says entry is free.

items.text: NEIGHBOURHOOD WATCH — Please report any suspicious activity to the local police on 101. Do not approach strangers. Keep your doors locked.
items.prompt: What does the notice tell residents to do if they see something suspicious?
opts: Approach the person and ask questions
items.rationale: The notice tells residents to call the police on 101 and not to approach strangers themselves.
